question_title: Will the U.S. Copyright Office publish a final, non-prepublication version of AI Report Part 3 before April 15, 2026?
summary: Part 3 of the Copyright Office AI report has been in prepublication status for 10 months since May 9, 2025. The day after its release, Trump fired Register Perlmutter, triggering ongoing litigation through the Supreme Court. The copyright.gov/ai/ page still shows Part 3 as prepublication with a vague promise of future finalization. There are no signals of imminent finalization, and only 30 days remain. While the report needs only cosmetic changes to finalize, the institutional paralysis and political hostility make this unlikely in the near term.

factors.description: Part 3 has been in prepublication status for 10 months (May 2025 to March 2026), suggesting institutional inertia or inability to finalize. Status quo strongly favors continued prepublication.

Copyright Office leadership in turmoil: Register fired by Trump the day after Part 3 released, ongoing litigation through Supreme Court. This makes routine administrative actions like finalizing a report much harder.
No news or signal of imminent finalization in any search results. No announcements, no press releases, no leaks about forthcoming final version.
Only 30 days remain until the April 15 deadline — a short window given no momentum toward finalization.
The page itself says final version will be published 'in the future' — acknowledging intent but without timeline. This is mildly positive but extremely vague.
The report requires no substantive changes to finalize — it's purely a relabeling/formatting task. This is technically easy if someone prioritizes it.
Trump administration may be actively hostile to this report (which found AI training often doesn't qualify as fair use), creating political disincentive to finalize.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 12% probability (NO)**

Key reasoning:
- **10 months of stasis**: Part 3 has been labeled "prepublication" since May 9, 2025, with no movement toward a final version
- **Institutional chaos**: The Register of Copyrights was fired by Trump the day after Part 3 was released, triggering litigation that's still at the Supreme Court level
- **No signals**: Zero indication from any source that finalization is imminent
- **Short window**: Only 30 days remain, and there's no momentum
- **Minor positive**: The report only needs cosmetic changes (relabeling), and the page acknowledges a final version will come "in the future" — but 10 months without action despite this being trivially easy suggests the barriers are political, not technical
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the U.S. Copyright Office publish a final, non-prepublication version of AI Report Part 3 before April 15, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42636)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the U.S. Copyright Office’s Copyright and Artificial Intelligence page at https://www.copyright.gov/ai/ posts a final, non-prepublication Part 3 report before April 15, 2026. Otherwise, it resolves to no.
fine_print: A document clearly labeled prepublication does not count. A final PDF or equivalent official final posting for Part 3 counts even if the substantive text is unchanged.
